name: Predication / Masking

x.executionModel: Conditional work is executed on all lanes but committed only where a per-lane predicate (mask) is true, replacing data-dependent branches with masked operations.
x.dataOrg: Works on vectors with a parallel boolean mask; SoA / uniform layouts maximise useful lanes.
x.hwConstraints: Both branches’ work may be computed; benefit depends on branch cost vs mask waste and on hardware mask support (AVX-512 k-masks, SVE, GPU predicate registers).
x.compilerSupport: Compilers if-convert small branches automatically; intrinsics expose masks explicitly.
x.portability: Native masks on AVX-512/SVE/GPUs; emulated (blend) on older SIMD.
x.expectedBenefit: Removes unpredictable branches from hot loops, keeping the pipeline / warp full.
bottlenecks: Executing both paths wastes work when masks are sparse
Blend emulation cost on older ISAs
divergenceRisks: Heavy divergence makes predication slower than branching
Side-effecting work under a false mask
x.workloadShapes: Short conditionals in tight numeric loops, GPU kernels with mild divergence.

def: Turning data-dependent branches into branchless masked operations, so SIMD lanes or GPU threads stay in lockstep by computing conditionally and committing per-lane.
problem: Branches inside vectorised / SIMT loops cause misprediction or warp divergence, stalling the pipeline.
mechanism: Instead of branching, both outcomes are computed and a per-lane predicate mask selects which results are kept (or which lanes write). This keeps the vector/warp converged at the cost of doing some discarded work.
benefits: Eliminates unpredictable branches in hot loops
limits: Needs hardware mask support to be cheap
failures: Predicating expensive both-paths where a branch would skip work
Executing side effects on masked-off lanes
whenNot: Branches are well-predicted and skip large work
Highly divergent control flow (let it branch)
myths: "Branchless is always faster" — predication wastes work; it wins only when branches are costly/divergent
